Despite my low score, it's maybe still worth a buy at 10 bucks. But my two biggest gripes are:

1. The map is about 80 to 90% water/ocean. The world is big, but there's not much in it.

2. You cannot refuel in a sim style way. Landing at airports does not refuel you and there are no refueling zones. You basically have to reset. From what I have read in forums, the devs have stated they refuse to implement this feature. I guess the number of people who care about this is small?

Aside from those two issues, planes can be kind of frustrating as there are tons of community created planes you can download, but a lot of them aren't very satisfying to fly.

Ultrawings 2 is still the most satisfying casual flight game on the Quest thus far, as there is a lot to be offered. Lots of different arcade style missions, plus a free flight mode where you can take off and land at any airport you purchased across islands, land to refuel, and the flexibility allows you to route yourself however you want. The world is a lot smaller, but there's more to do.

This app is profound and a gift from the gods. At last a chance to fly airplane using the stick as well as the yoke.

The planes themselves are Nintendo 64 graphics. The dials in some of the planes and the photorealistic compositing in the natural scenery is quite well done.

This app is profound and life changing.

Place a flashlight or a toy kaleidoscope tube or the like through the strap of the right hand VR hand grip. Then place the entire set up under your legs stretch straight out and not too far forward or too far backward off course. You can intuit it. 
This creates a structural realism. 
For the structurally realistic yoke experience on Simple Planes VR, place a whatever through both straps of the handgrips. Also holding either end of a chopstick with each hand, say between the middle fingers and the ring fingers adds a lateral structural realness. It reduces freedom of movement consistent with structure.

I know that development's probably already moved on to SimplePlanes 2 and Juno, but please update SimplePlanes VR one last time. The game was made a while ago, so it's optimized for very low-end devices. There should be more graphics settings and also other stuff like the ability to spawn custom planes instead of just stock planes like base SimplePlanes. Also, while base SimplePlanes has new lever types, SimplePlanes VR still only has one lever type (the default one), which causes many planes to not have levers (in the VR version). Also, maybe add some QoL changes, like a search bar and glove color/transparency customization.

The base game is a bit lacking, but the mods are where this game shines. From the simplest cargo planes which simply require you to hit the throttle, to a accurate Cessna which requires the proper fuel to air ratio to fly, this game truly has everything. Fly your favorite plane (MIG because MIGs are simply superior) through a massive open world, attempting landings which quickly evolve into emergency take offs and emergency landings. Bash your head against your cockpit as you attempt to get a Cessna in the air. Pull out your emergency pilot pistol as you realize you completely overshot your destination while getting a drink of water and are now hopelessly lost. Blast danger zone through your headset as your F-15 quickly goes out of control since you're left handed. 

You'll probably have fun. And probably recreate some historical events.
So many planes to choose from and explore islands, I would recommend it

Now this is going to be a little lengthy review. So reading this fully is not expected.

To start.
I’ve played this game for a long while now.(around 3 years or maybe longer from what I can remember). Both the mobile version and pc version. So for a game that allows such creativity and flexibility especially with its community.
This game is honestly cool for what it is and where it’s coming from. 

Now I understand that games need to be optimized and are limited to quest headsets due to quest being a more compact and mobile experience to VR. So I get that trying to make a simple sandbox game with a complex concept work with a system that is not designed for crazy graphics, codes, and wide flexibility. So getting all the details is pretty much impossible.

BUT the thing I do love about this game in its entirety, is the community and their ability to create. There are some INSANELY awesome builders who put time and extreme effort and dedication into every detail of their creations. Which is something I find really cool.

Yet here, it comes at a cost. The downside of have such a creative and detailed community is that since quest is so limited, not all creations are available (or able to be used without the risk of crashing).

Which makes sense.

Because knowing how crazy accurate builders  can be when it comes to vehicles. Limiting high quality and more detailed vehicles that have a high block count, is a must in order to keep the game running smoothly. So limiting vehicles is bound to happen. 

This is how the MOBILE version of the game works. Higher quality creations are not restricted (nor truly limited) but instead, when downloading a creation that exceeds a certain amount of blocks will automatically display a prompt warning you that such a creation can and/or will crash your game. But still giving you the option to go through with downloading them. Thus never truly restricting you from downloading extremely detailed builds.

And having a cool looking creation download and then finding out that it only crashes your game can be frustrating. ESPECIALLY when you can’t find something close to it that won’t crash your game.  

Another downside is the inability to create while in VR. Which would be cool but understandably a challenge. But still would be cool to have.

With each version being around $10 each. It can be a real debate of whether you should or shouldn’t get the all the versions of the game. It mainly depends on what you as a player and builder enjoy and engage with the game.

Now don’t get me wrong. I’ve loved this game (and will continue to love it) since the day I first got it. All because of the ability to create vehicles with either a logical and strategic plan or literally nothing but trial and error. Making this game listed as one of my personal favorites. Whether to brainstorm and create or just to play and have fun.

Therefore the game is really good for how it is. Would ABSOLUTELY recommend if you’re looking for a fun way to fly and drive crazy vehicles, or even make your own. Both the PC, and the mobile versions are perfect for making really cool creations and stuff. 
The VR version on the other hand, is more better for just wanting to have a fun FPV using those cool creations. 
 
Overall, the VR version (especially for quest) passes along with the PC and mobile versions. With literally endless possibilities of fun,(THIS IS EXCLUDING WHEN PLAYING WITH FRIENDS! Which makes the game even more fun. Making absolutely hilarious moments inevitably. Story for another day.) this game is something that you can go to and just have a blast. 

Now stop reading this review, get the game, and have fun. Explore the endless builds that people have made and just enjoy the game. 

Thank you for taking your time to read this review. 

And an even bigger thanks to the devs for make such a simple game that pulled together such a cool community. -SR

Where to start? Their aircraft models are wrong wrong wrong.

For instance : the Cessna 172 model it is beyond wrong.

 It's really weird.  you have a flaps indicator but you can't put in any flaps because there's no control, ( how are you supposed to do a soft field or short field TO or put in flaps for final ?) the mag selector is wrong, the trims are okay but most modern Cessna 172 's -  even the r-types will have a trim Control located on the yoke, the aircraft has so many issues ! you can do nothing with the VORs , you can not calibrate your gyro compass , you can do nothing with the coms , you can do nothing with the transponder.  The mixture control and throttle are okay ...I GUESS but the plane flies goofy . And that is only one of the planes  I tried . $10 , yea I guess Ill keep it but there is a lot of issues . 

The programers need to consult with a pilot before making something so goofy . The awful program ultra - wings which is also bad is 10 x better than this silly thing . You gotta know going in that one day a pilot might try your program and yes , I get that its a game but it is way TF off the mark .  Not even a beta , flight sim2 on an apple 2 plus is better than this . The graphics were garbage but it has the correct controls and flight characteristics of the 172 and the transponder works , the com's work, the VORs work ,the flaps work. This is the goofiest piece of sw I have ever purchased.

This is a great flight sim if you don’t have a pc, now we need to make multiplayer then it would be even better🙏, other players can fly their own planes or if there is more than one seat in a plane, helicopter etc you can ride with them. Creators of SimplePlanes if you read this than just know, your game doesn’t need any graphics it’s really good, Thank you 😁

This is a great app! I got my private ticket back in 08 for single engine, but it just got too expensive to fly for recreation. This allows me to fly so many different aircraft at an affordable price 😃. Still in development, and some of the cockpits are a bit cluttered. Too many switches too close together. Makes it difficult to grab the right one sometimes. But still A LOT of fun! Well worth the $10!!

Dev’s:

Keep up the great work!

reviews.review_title: Good but with one important suggestion
reviews.review_description: I love the game but there is thing that would make this game a lot better: The ability to change the color/brightness of your hands. The bright white hands contrast significantly with the dark interior of the cockpits which creates horrible godrays. These are super noticable and are really distracting in game. I know this just the effect of the lenses and isn't really the games fault but being able to change the color and brightness of the hands would help alleviate this problem.
